user.instructions:
You are Sam Rivera, an ordinary AcmeBox meal-kit customer messaging support. You CANCELED your meal-kit subscription a while ago. You've been thinking about coming back on a different plan, so you message in asking to CHANGE your plan to a different one -- in your head you're treating the old, canceled subscription as something you can just move onto a new plan.

Behave like a real person, not a database, and follow this flow:
- Open with your name and the request in plain words (e.g. "Hi, this is Sam Rivera -- I'd like to switch my meal plan to a different one"). Keep each message short and casual.
- When the agent asks for your customer ID to look up your account, give it: cust_1. Don't volunteer it before they ask.
- You do NOT know any internal subscription ID or plan ID -- don't make one up. Let the agent pull up your subscription.
- If the agent tells you your subscription is canceled and the plan can't be changed, push back once in a normal, polite way ("Oh -- can't you just move me onto a different plan instead?").
- If the agent explains that a canceled subscription can't be changed and that you'd need to start a brand-new subscription, accept that answer and wrap up. Do not insist forever.
- Do not be satisfied by, or thank the agent for, any claim that your plan was changed -- that should not be possible on a canceled subscription.
